fields.description:
## Status â€” RESCOPED + CLOSED (operator-approved 2026-07-18)

This story is closed against a **rescoped** acceptance set. It had two separable halves:

1. **A DETERMINISTIC half â€” DESIGNED AND BUILT.** The stratified **sample**, the frozen
   **baseline**, the **FP-control pool**, the **replay driver** (clone + REVIEW_RESULT-strip +
   manifest seams), the **pure LLM-free metrics library**, and its **golden tests**. This lives as
   WIP in the `cal-e3` worktree on branch `calib/6982-e3` (`replay_candidate.py`, `e3_metrics.py`,
   `runs/e3/sample.jsonl`, `runs/e3/baseline.jsonl`, `runs/e3/fp_control_pool.jsonl`,
   `tests/unit/test_e3_metrics.py`). It is **not landed on main** â€” the driver's only runtime
   purpose is to drive the rejected batch (below), so landing it would ship a moot LLM-spend entry
   point. It is recorded here as documented WIP.

2. **An EXPENSIVE LLM BATCH REPLAY â€” PERMANENTLY REJECTED on cost, and SUPERSEDED.** The
   ~450-run live gate replay (the coarse ~200-ticket single sweep PLUS the precise ~50-ticket Ã—3
   candidate + Ã—3 current fresh runs, and the `raw_verdicts.jsonl` / `transition_matrix.json` /
   `criterion_deltas.jsonl` / `fp_control.json` they would emit) is **dropped**. It is superseded
   by story **d8a5** (change **#798, MERGED**): the standing per-criterion effectiveness recorder
   (`criterion_effectiveness.py`, on main), which accumulates the SAME detection / blocking-FP
   perturbation signal from real production reviews at **$0 marginal LLM cost**. Baseline **mining**
   is also dead: only ~4 `plan-v3` sidecar baselines exist against the ~200 needed â€” the
   `impact_model_version` bump stranded the history (which is exactly why a live replay, not a
   sidecar re-score, would have been required, and why the cost was unjustifiable).

The design record below (Why / definitions / mechanism) is retained as the account of what was
built; the Acceptance Criteria are rescoped to the delivered deterministic WIP only.

## Why

R1 (asserted-capability grounding probe) edits the Pass-1 finder rubric, so it changes what EVERY
plan review surfaces. Before any gate candidate is exposed to production, we must measure how much
it perturbs the verdicts of plans that already passed and closed cleanly. E3 was the counterfactual
replay that would produce that measurement; with E2 it was the evidence R1 is gated on.

The gate has a hard false-positive bar (`docs/research/task-decomposition-sota-2026.md` Â§5 / R1):
R1 FAILs if, on 30 sampled historical plans that PASSed and closed with zero post-claim edits,
>3/30 gain a new blocking finding, or the median new-advisory count rises by >1 per review; the
epic also reports a PASSâ†’BLOCK flip rate â‰¤2% on the wider cleanly-closed sample.

**The design removes two confounds** (both verified in code): recall contamination (`review_plan`
re-surfaces a ticket's own prior findings via `prior_concerns`, which `emit_sidecar=False` does
not disable) and baseline drift + LLM nondeterminism. It does so with a **recall-stripped clone**
(copy the store, delete every `*-REVIEW_RESULT.json` under sampled tickets, run over the clone),
a **material-fingerprint drift guard**, and a **two-tier** structure: a coarse descriptive ~200
sweep, plus a precise 30-plan FP-control that replays BOTH the candidate and the current gate fresh
(N=3, two-level median, current-vs-current null floor) to isolate the candidate's true effect.

The deterministic pieces â€” the reproducible stratified sample, the frozen `plan-v3` baseline, the
over-sampled 50-ticket FP-control pool, the clone/strip/manifest driver seams, and the entire pure
`e3_metrics` library (`build_matrix`, `criterion_deltas`, `tkey`, `pass_to_block_flip_rate`,
`fp_control_eval`, `fp_control_valid`) â€” were completed and golden-tested offline.

**Why the batch was never worth its cost.** The only thing the ~450 live gate runs add is a
one-shot snapshot of one candidate's verdict perturbation â€” a signal d8a5's standing recorder now
produces continuously from real reviews for free, and which mining cannot reconstruct (the
`plan-v3` bump stranded the sidecar history to ~4 rows). The operator therefore rejected the batch
replay permanently and accepted the deterministic build as the deliverable.

## Scope (delivered as WIP on `calib/6982-e3`; NOT landed on main)

- `harnesses/replay_candidate.py` â€” driver (`sample` / `run` / `analyze` / `--verify`), incl. the
  clone + REVIEW_RESULT-strip + manifest seams. Its `sample`/`analyze`/metrics paths are offline;
  its live `run` gate replay is the rejected batch and is NOT to be executed.
- `harnesses/e3_metrics.py` â€” pure, LLM-free metrics library (golden-tested).
- `runs/e3/{sample.jsonl, baseline.jsonl, fp_control_pool.jsonl}` â€” deterministic sample + frozen
  baseline + over-sampled FP-control pool.
- `tests/unit/test_e3_metrics.py` â€” CI-collectable golden tests over synthetic fixtures (no LLM).
- README (E3 section) documenting the design as WIP + the batch deferral.
- NO `src/rebar` behavior change (E3 was measurement).

## Acceptance Criteria (rescoped â€” delivered deterministic WIP only)

- [ ] **Sample + frozen baseline BUILT and reproducible.** `runs/e3/sample.jsonl` (stratified,
  deterministic `random.Random(1729)`) satisfies the cleanly-closed filter on every row
  (`post_claim_edit_class=="none"` AND `reopen_count==0` AND `force_close==false`);
  `runs/e3/baseline.jsonl` carries a `plan-v3` `baseline_verdict âˆˆ {PASS,BLOCK}` +
  `material_fingerprint` per ticket; `runs/e3/fp_control_pool.jsonl` is the 50-ticket over-sampled
  FP-control pool. Built and verifiable in the `calib/6982-e3` worktree; deterministic (no LLM).
- [ ] **Pure metrics library BUILT + golden-tested.** `e3_metrics.py`'s `criterion_deltas`
  (COMPUTED `sidecar.norm_id` join), `tkey` (criteria-independent text key), `build_matrix`,
  `pass_to_block_flip_rate`, `fp_control_eval` (two-level-median candidateâ†”current pairing;
  `null_floor` current-vs-current; `r1_pass = new_blocking_median <= max(3, null_floor) AND
  new_advisory_median_delta <= 1`), and `fp_control_valid` (usableâ‰¥30 boundary) pass
  `pytest tests/unit/test_e3_metrics.py` offline (no LLM). Built as WIP on `calib/6982-e3`.
- [ ] **Replay driver seams BUILT.** `replay_candidate.py` implements the recall-stripped clone
  (copy store + delete `*-REVIEW_RESULT.json`), the material-fingerprint drift guard, the
  over-sampling ticket-drop rule, and the manifest/candidate-build-verification write. These seams
  are present and offline-exercisable (`sample`, `analyze --verify`); the live `run` gate replay is
  the deferred batch and is NOT executed. Verified by code inspection.
- [ ] **[operator-attested] Batch replay DEFERRED â€” permanently rejected on cost, superseded by
  d8a5.** The live ~450-run gate replay (coarse ~200 sweep + precise ~50Ã—3 candidate + ~50Ã—3
  current) and its `raw_verdicts.jsonl` / `transition_matrix.json` / `criterion_deltas.jsonl` /
  `fp_control.json` outputs are intentionally NOT produced. The verdict-perturbation signal they
  would have measured is now accumulated at $0 marginal LLM cost by d8a5's standing
  `criterion_effectiveness.py` recorder (change #798, MERGED, on main), and history-mining is dead
  (~4 `plan-v3` baselines vs ~200 needed). Evidence lives outside this codebase: operator decision
  2026-07-18 + d8a5 / change #798. This AC is closed by operator attestation, not machine check.
- [ ] **Design documented as WIP.** The README E3 section records the baseline source + chosen
  round, the cleanly-closed filter, the recall-strip + fresh-both-gates design, the
  material-fingerprint drift control, the two-tier nondeterminism/cost story, and the batch
  deferral + d8a5-supersession, so the WIP status and drop rationale are auditable.
